questions.question: What is the attention context vector at decoder step t?
options: A weighted average of encoder hidden states where the weights come from a query-key score
questions.explanation: Context c_t = Σ α_{t,i} * h_i — a step-dependent weighted average of all encoder states, not just the last one. The weights α change at every decoder step.

questions.question: In Bahdanau (additive) attention, what role does the vector v_a play?
options: It is the projection that turns the attention-dim hidden combination into a scalar score per encoder position
questions.explanation: v_a dot-products with tanh(W_a s + U_a h) to collapse a d_attn-dimensional vector into a scalar score — the projection that makes each encoder position comparable.

questions.question: In Luong's 'dot' attention variant, what constraint must hold?
options: Decoder state and encoder state must share the same dimensionality (d_s == d_h)
questions.explanation: Luong dot uses s^T h with no projection matrix, so dimensions must match exactly — skip dot and use the 'general' variant when the encoder is bidirectional.

questions.question: Which Q/K/V mapping describes classical (Bahdanau/Luong) attention?
options: Q, K, V are three independent learned projections of the source
Q is random, K and V are learned
Q from encoder, K and V from decoder
Q = decoder state; K and V = encoder states (same tensor)
questions.explanation: In classical attention, keys and values are both the encoder states (same tensor); transformers split K and V via separately learned projections — the key architectural divergence.

questions.question: Why is reporting raw attention weights as 'explanation' considered fragile?
options: They are too small to plot
Research (e.g. Jain and Wallace, 2019) showed attention distributions can be permuted without changing predictions on some tasks
questions.explanation: Attention weights correlate with predictions but are not faithful causal explanations — always verify with ablation or counterfactual checks before reporting them as reasoning evidence.

questions.question: When adapting `additive_attention` toward transformer self-attention, which is the key conceptual pivot?
options: Querying a sequence against itself with separately learned Q, K, and V projections, run in parallel heads
questions.explanation: Self-attention queries the same sequence, splits K and V into separate learned projections, and runs attention in parallel across multiple heads — the math of additive_attention remains; only the source of Q/K/V changes.
